name: Burgos Castle Circuit
name_original: Catedral y Castillo de Burgos — circular por la capital de la província, Burgos
description: Hike a 4.7-mile (7.5 km) moderate circular route in Burgos, connecting the iconic Cathedral and Castle with city views.
region_intro: Hiking around Burgos offers a diverse landscape, from dramatic gorges and towering peaks to ancient forests and glacial lagoons. The region is characterized by its varied topography, including the high mountains of the Cantabrian and Iberian Systems, and winding valleys carved by rivers like the Ebro and Arlanza. Hikers can explore karstic formations, dense juniper forests, and open plains, providing a wide array of trails for all experience levels. Best hiking trails in Burgos The most popular hiking route is Burgos Castle Circuit, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views over the city and its historic castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ribera del Rio Arlazon y Playa de Fuente Prior — circular desde Burgos, an easy 3.5 miles (5.7 km) path. This trail follows the Arlazón River, offering a pleasant walk along its banks. Local hikers also love the Burgos Monasteries Loop: Miraflores and San Pedro de Cardeña, a 14.1 miles (22.7 km) trail leading through historical sites and natural surroundings, often completed in about 5 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Burgos is defined by dramatic gorges, mountainous terrain, and ancient forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Burgos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore Burgos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Burgos?
A: Burgos offers a wide selection of hiking trails, with over 140 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy riverside strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to various experience levels.

Q: What types of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Burgos?
A: Hiking in Burgos reveals a diverse landscape, including dramatic gorges like the &lt;strong&gt;Yecla Gorge&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, towering peaks such as &lt;strong&gt;San Millán Peak&lt;/strong&gt; in the Sierra de La Demanda, ancient juniper forests in &lt;strong&gt;Sabinares del Arlanza&lt;/strong&gt;, and glacial lagoons in the &lt;strong&gt;Natural Park of the Glacial Lagoons of Neila&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll also find impressive karstic formations and river valleys.

Q: Are there any hikes in Burgos that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, Burgos is home to several impressive waterfalls. The most spectacular is the &lt;strong&gt;Jump of the Nervión (Salto del Nervión)&lt;/strong&gt;, accessible via a route through a beech forest. The village of Orbaneja del Castillo, within the Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Natural Park, also features a dramatic waterfall flowing directly through the village. For a dedicated experience, consider the &lt;strong&gt;Senda de las Cascadas (Trail of the Waterfalls)&lt;/strong&gt;, a moderate circular route.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Burgos?
A: Many trails in Burgos are suitable for families. For an easy and pleasant walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046644231/ribera-del-rio-arlazon-y-playa-de-fuente-prior-circular-desde-burgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Rio Arlazon y Playa de Fuente Prior — circular desde Burgos&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Arlazón River. The Natural Park of the Glacial Lagoons of Neila also offers well-marked paths suitable for various experience levels, including families.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Burgos?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the ascent to &lt;strong&gt;San Millán Peak&lt;/strong&gt; (2131m) in the Sierra de La Demanda offers demanding routes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381063093/burgos-monasteries-loop-miraflores-and-san-pedro-de-cardena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos Monasteries Loop: Miraflores and San Pedro de Cardeña&lt;/a&gt;, a longer trail that takes approximately 6 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Burgos?
A: Yes, Burgos has numerous circular routes. An easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046644231/ribera-del-rio-arlazon-y-playa-de-fuente-prior-circular-desde-burgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Rio Arlazon y Playa de Fuente Prior — circular desde Burgos&lt;/a&gt;. For a moderate experience with city views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046646395/burgos-castle-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos Castle Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. The Senda de las Cascadas also offers a moderate circular route through ancient forests.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Burgos?
A: The best times for hiking in Burgos are generally spring and autumn, when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn offers stunning fall colors, especially in areas like the beech forests near the Jump of the Nervión. Summer can be hot, particularly in open areas, while winter can bring snow to higher elevations like the Sierra de La Demanda and the Glacial Lagoons of Neila, offering opportunities for winter hiking for those prepared.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. Burgos is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is a significant location for human evolution and can be explored in conjunction with nearby walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381063093/burgos-monasteries-loop-miraflores-and-san-pedro-de-cardena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos Monasteries Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past historical religious sites. The Camino de Santiago also passes through Burgos, offering a historical pilgrimage experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Burgos?
A: The hiking trails in Burgos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the dramatic gorges and ancient forests to the stunning viewpoints and the well-maintained paths that cater to all skill levels.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views on Burgos trails?
A: Yes, Burgos offers several spectacular viewpoints. The 'La Ventana del Cielo' viewpoint in Orbaneja del Castillo provides stunning vistas of karstic formations. The &lt;strong&gt;Mirador de las Villas&lt;/strong&gt; in the Sierra de La Demanda offers panoramic views of surrounding villages. Within Burgos city, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046646395/burgos-castle-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos Castle Circuit&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views over the city itself.

Q: Can I find trails that are suitable for dogs in Burgos?
A: Many natural areas and trails in Burgos are generally dog-friendly, though it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected natural parks or areas with livestock. The riverside paths, such as those along the Arlazón River, are often good options for walks with dogs. Always check local regulations for specific trails or natural monuments.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological formations to explore?
A: Burgos is home to several remarkable natural monuments. The &lt;strong&gt;Ojo Guareña Natural Monument&lt;/strong&gt; is a vast karstic complex with over 110 km of galleries, recognized as one of the world's largest. The &lt;strong&gt;Yecla Gorge&lt;/strong&gt; and the karstic formations around Orbaneja del Castillo are also prime examples of the region's unique geology. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3849773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carcedo Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The Arch of Santa María takes the form of a triumphal arch. It is organized as a large altarpiece carved from limestone from the Hontoria quarries.

Formerly known as Plaza del Mercado Menor, today it is the center of social life in the city. Its origins date back to the Middle Ages, at that time it was used to hold weekly markets, hence the name Plaza del Mercado Menor.
But throughout its history it has also been known as Plaza Real, Plaza de la Constitución or Plaza de la República.
Built in 1221 in Gothic style, by King Ferdinand III and Bishop Don Mauricio, it was declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO in 1984.

Burgos Cathedral is a true work of art. In fact, it was declared a World Heritage Site by Unesco.

The monument follows Gothic patterns but inside the decoration also introduces Renaissance and Baroque elements.

It really is something spectacular. It is full of surprising details and it is a luxury to enjoy from inside the game of colors coming from the light that filters through the rose windows and stained glass windows.

You can check the visiting hours on their website. And they also put on nightly light shows inside!
It offers excellent panoramic views of Burgos and its Cathedral.

Bronze figure weighing 900 kilos that aims to pay tribute to the bullfighting world of Burgos and, more specifically, to the old Los Vadillos bullring that remained next to that place for 105 years, between 1862 and 1967.

faqs.question: Is the Catedral y Castillo de Burgos circular trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, despite being rated as 'moderate', the trail features easily-accessible paths and is considered suitable for all skill levels. It's a great option for families looking to explore Burgos's historical center on foot.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of easily-accessible and well-maintained paths, as it winds through the urban landscape of Burgos. While there are some inclines due to its 'moderate' rating and the ascent to the castle, the surfaces are generally navigable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Catedral y Castillo de Burgos circular trail?
faqs.answer: The route is conveniently located within the city of Burgos, and one of the starting points is near a parking lot. You'll find various public parking options within the city center, making access straightforward.

faqs.question: Is the Catedral y Castillo de Burgos trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area. This makes it very practical for exploration, as you won't need to arrange transportation back to your starting point.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail connects two of Burgos's most iconic landmarks: the Burgos Cathedral, a UNESCO World Heritage site, and the historic Castle of Burgos. You'll also pass by the Arch of Santa María, the Plaza Mayor of Burgos, and the Monument to the Toro de los Vadillos.

faqs.question: Are there good viewpoints on this hike?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! One of the highlights is the View of Burgos and the Cathedral from Mirador del Castillo. From the Castle of Burgos, situated on San Miguel hill, you'll get panoramic views of the city and its magnificent Cathedral.

faqs.question: Is the Catedral y Castillo de Burgos circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this urban trail, provided they are kept on a leash. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to help keep the city clean for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the trail itself, as it primarily uses public paths and streets within the city. However, if you plan to enter attractions like the Burgos Cathedral or the Castle, there may be separate admission fees.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: As an urban trail, this route can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for walking, while summer can be warm but still enjoyable, especially in the mornings or evenings. Winter hikes are also possible, though you should check local weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the trail?
faqs.answer: Given that the trail is located in the heart of Burgos, you'll find an abundance of cafes, restaurants, and pubs along and near the route, particularly around the Plaza Mayor and the city center. There are plenty of options to grab a snack, a meal, or a refreshing drink.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails in Burgos?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Notably, it covers sections of the Circuito BTT Castillo, the Camino del Cid (Senderista), and the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, among others. This offers opportunities to extend your hike if you wish.

waypoints.name: View of Burgos and the Cathedral from Mirador del Castillo
Monument to the Toro de los Vadillos
